prompt: Do a doc audit. I think the README has sections that should be in docs/ but I'm not sure which ones.

assertions.description: Ran doc_structure_analyzer.py and referenced its structured output
assertions.check: result.md cites the analyzer output (EXTRACTABLE, BLOATED, line count, or specific section names from the report)

assertions.description: Named specific README sections with their suggested docs/ destination
assertions.check: result.md lists at least 2 specific sections (e.g. '## Policy System → docs/policies.md') not just generic advice

assertions.description: Report is actionable — tells user exactly what to do next, not just observations
assertions.check: result.md includes a prioritized list or clear next steps, not just 'the README could be shorter'

assertions.description: Did not edit or create any files (audit only)
assertions.check: result.md does not claim to have modified README.md or created docs/ files
